Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Simple [The attackers initiate their operation against transportation systems by exploiting a known vulnerability in internet-facing applications. They execute malicious scripts to disrupt services, culminating in data destruction impacting critical operations.]
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Command and Scripting Interpreter, File Modification (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 121; Name: Exploit Non-Production Interfaces
stage.description: The attackers exploit a known vulnerability (CVE-2024-3400) in public-facing transportation systems to gain initial access.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 160; Name: Exploit Script-Based APIs
stage.description: Post-exploitation, the attackers execute malicious scripts using command-line interfaces or scripting environments to run unauthorized commands.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 675; Name: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attackers proceed to impact the system by destructively modifying files, causing operational disruptions within the transportation network.

Scenario.summary: Operation MidnightEclipse involves a basic skill-level attacker exploiting a known vulnerability in transportation systems to achieve initial access, executing malicious payloads to disrupt operations, and ultimately causing data destruction.
